advantages: Automatic run IDs and persistent history — no manual spreadsheet tracking of hyperparameters and scores.
Side-by-side run comparison on params, metrics, and artifacts speeds model selection after sweeps.
Artifact storage ties each model file to the exact run that produced it — clear lineage for debugging and deployment.
Standardized logging APIs (MLflow, W&B) integrate with sklearn, PyTorch, Hugging Face, and CI pipelines.
W&B live dashboards and MLflow metric history help catch training failures and overfitting early.
disadvantages: Setup overhead: tracking URI, credentials, and logging wrappers must be maintained across scripts and environments.
Inconsistent logging conventions (metric names, missing seeds) make comparison tables misleading or incomplete.
Artifact and metric storage grows with run volume — requires retention policies and optional paid cloud tiers.
MLflow UI is functional but less interactive than W&B for large teams; W&B cloud dependency may conflict with strict data residency.
Logging the wrong split metric (train instead of val) can still look rigorous while selecting overfit runs.
